question_text: Which statement about gravity is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravity only acts on objects on the Earth's surface

options.label: B
options.text: Gravity acts between all objects that have mass

options.label: D
options.text: Gravity is stronger on the Moon than on Earth

explanation: Gravity is a universal force that acts between all objects with mass, not just on Earth's surface. It is a force of attraction that pulls objects together, and it is stronger where there is more mass, so Earth's gravity is stronger than the Moon's.
